name: Kainuu (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the yellow field of the flag
the coniferous forest and the wavy waterline, evoking Kainuu's woodlands and lakes

description: A yellow flag charged with a green stylised forest of fir trees above a green wavy stripe, evoking the woodlands and waters of Kainuu.
history: Kainuu became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform. Its flag depicts a stylised coniferous forest above a wavy waterline, referring to the region's extensive forests and lakes in eastern Finland.

features.role: a row of stylised fir (spruce) trees forming a forest across the flag
features.symbolism: the coniferous forests that cover much of Kainuu

features.role: a green wavy stripe below the forest
features.symbolism: the lakes and waters of the region
